# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.135.0/http/server.ts";

const server = serve({ port: 8080 });
console.log("Listening on http://localhost:8080");

for await (const req of server) {
    const modulePath = req.url?.split('?module=')[1] || Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");
    
    if (modulePath) {
        try {
            // Dynamic import with user specified path
            const mod = await import(`file://${Deno.cwd()}/${modulePath}`);
            
            req.respond({ body: `Module ${modulePath} loaded and ready to use.` });
        } catch (err) {
            console.error(err);
            req.respond({ status: 500, body: 'Failed to load module' });
        }
    } else {
      req.respond({ status: 400, body: "Bad Request: Missing 'module' query parameter or MODULE_PATH environment variable." });  
    }
}
